tweet_text: most fitness app users churn in 14 days. the ones who stay have one thing in common: a visible streak. not a personal best. not a PR. a number that goes up by 1 every day they show up. streak mechanics > feature lists.

discipline is doing the boring thing 44 days in a row. whether it's showing up to the gym or pressing send on something you built. the reps are the same. the resistance is the same. the only difference is where you feel the burn.

337 automated scripts running daily. think of it as 337 sets on autopilot. but none of them count if you never show up to the gym. systems without action = reps without range of motion. full ROM or nothing.
progressive overload applies to everything. last week: 47 sites deployed. this week: 357. same engine, more volume. the principle doesn't change just because you leave the weight room.

your excuses don't burn calories. 289 cron jobs run every single day without asking permission. they don't negotiate with themselves about whether today is the day. they just execute. be the cron job.

the hardest rep is always the first one. in the gym it's getting off the couch. in business it's pressing send on the first cold email. 251 drafts sitting there. zero sent. day 44. the warmup set that never ends.
